properties.remark: SEVERAL SOURCES INCLUDING LAW ENFORCEMENT PERSONNEL AND A STORM SPOTTER REPORTED AT LEAST TWO WATERSPOUTS APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES SOUTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE AROUND 210 PM LOCAL. THE WATERSPOUTS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH A NORTHWEST TO SOUTHEAST ORIENTED CLOUD LINE THAT WAS PRODUCING SHOWERS AT THE TIME. PICTURES SUBMITTED BY THE SOURCES INDICATE AT LEAST ONE OF THE WATERSPOUTS HAD A SPRAY RING. THEIR DURATION WAS LESS THAN 10 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KTS WAS REPORTED BY THE WEATHERFLOW STATION ON SMITH SHOAL. GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A SQUALL LINE MOVING NE AT 25 TO 30 KTS.
BOATER AT ANCHOR OFF OF NORTH ROOSEVELT BOULEVARD, GULF SIDE WATERS, REPORTED AN ESTIMATED 40 MPH WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH A SQUALL LINE MOVING NE AT 25 TO 30 KTS.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KTS WAS REPORTED BY THE WEATHERFLOW STATION LOCATED AT THE KEY WEST COAST GUARD STATION.
A 37 KT WIND GUST WAS REPORTED BY A DAVIS WEATHER STATION ON CUDJOE KEY ASSOCIATED WITH A NE MOVING SQUALL LINE.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A GUST OF 38 KTS ASSOCIATED WITH A BAND OF FAST MOVING SHOWERS
THE LONG KEY CMAN STATION RECORDED A GUST OF 37 KTS ASSOCIATED WITHIN A BAND OF FAST MOVING SHOWERS
THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION RECORDED A GUST OF 34 KTS ASSOCIATED WITHIN A BAND OF FAST MOVING SHOWERS
THE WEATHERFLOW ANEMOMETER LOCATED ON SMITH SHOAL LIGHT REPORTED A 44 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH THE OUTFLOW OF A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING ACROSS THE AREA.

NUMEROUS TREES REPORTED DOWN ALONG LONG BEACH ROAD ON THE SOUTH END OF BIG PINE KEY. SOME TREES WERE 18 INCHES DIAMETER. DAMAGE TO PORCHES AND AWNINGS ALSO REPORTED IN THAT AREA. APPROXIMATE TIME OF EVENT WAS BETWEEN 1030 PM AND 1100 PM THURSDAY EVENING. EVENT IS BEING INVESTIGATED AS POSSIBLE WATERSPOUT OR TORNADO.

UPDATED INFORMATION ON WIND DAMAGE EVENT AT SOUTH END OF BIG PINE KEY ALONG LONG BEACH ROAD. CORRECT APPROXIMATE TIME OF EVENT WAS BETWEEN 130 AND 200 AM EARLY FRIDAY MORNING. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN WITH SOME UPROOTED AND OTHERS SNAPPED OFF. DAMAGE TO MULTIPLE SCREEN PORCHES AND AWNINGS...ALSO SECTION OF SIDING BLOWN OFF ONE HOUSE. 200 GALLON WATER TANK WAS MOVED...PART OF ONE DOCK LOFTED INTO THE TREES. THIS EVENT REMAINS UNDER INVESTIGATION AS POSSIBLE TORNADO.
THE WEATHER FLOW ANEMOMETER LOCATED ON SMITH SHOAL LIGHT REPORTED A 37 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH THE OUTFLOW OF A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.

UPDATED INFORMATION ON WIND DAMAGE EVENT PREVIOUSLY RECORDED AS THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE NOW RECLASSIFIED AS AN EF0 TORNADO EVENT. BASED ON INTERVIEWS BETWEEN HOME OWNERS AND WFO KEY WEST METEOROLOGIST THE START TIME OF THE EVENT IS BEING CHANGED TO 315 AM.THE WFO KEY WEST METEOROLOGIST FOUND DAMAGE TO BE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED 3-SECOND WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 75 AND 80 MPH. A WATERSPOUT CAME ASHORE FROM THE WEST SOUTHWEST AT THE SOUTH END OF BIG PINE KEY ALONG LONG BEACH RD.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS WAS REPORTED BY MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS REPORTED BY MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS WAS REPORTED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION.
A RAPIDLY-MOVING SHOWER PRODUCED A SOUTHEAST TO SOUTH WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 255 PM EDT.
AT 215 PM EDT CAPTAIN JACK CARLSON OF TWO CONCHS CHARTERS OUT OF MARATHON REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 9 NAUTICAL MILES NW OF JOHN SAWYER BANK LIGHT MOVING NE AT 10 KTS.
AT 215 PM EDT CAPTAIN JACK CARLSON OF TWO CONCHS CHARTERS OUT OF MARATHON REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 9 NAUTICAL MILES NW OF JOHN SAWYER BANK LIGHT MOVING NE AT 10 KTS.

A SECOND WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED NEXT TO THE FIRST WATERSPOUT AT 216 PM EDT. CAPTAIN JACK CARLSON OF TWO CONCHS CHARTERS OUT OF MARATHON REPORTED A SECOND WATERSPOUT ABOUT 9 NAUTICAL MILES NW OF JOHN SAWYER BANK LIGHT MOVING NE AT 10 KTS.
OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A DECAYING WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES SE OF BOCA CHICA AT 450 PM EDT.

SPOTTER CALLED AT 515 PM AND REPORTED 3 WATERSPOUTS BETWEEN 400-415 PM EDT. TWO OF THE WATERSPOUTS WERE ROPES AND THE THIRD WAS MEDIUM SIZE. THEY ALL APPEARED TO BE BLUE, AND ALL 3 WATERSPOUTS WERE NEAR RAINSHAFTS WITH NO THUNDER/LIGHTNING ASSOCIATED. THE WATERSPOUTS WERE MOVING TOWARD THE SW AT ABOUT 10 MPH.
A PHOTO OF A WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT WAS SENT TO WFO KEY WEST VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.THE WATERSPOUT WAS LOCATED IN THE VICINITY OF COTTRELL KEY. THE DURATION OF THE WATERSPOUT IS UNKNOWN.

AN OFF DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST REPORTED 2 FUNNEL CLOUDS TO THE WEST OF KEY WEST. AN NWS METEOROLOGIST ON DUTY VERIFIED FUNNEL CLOUDS AS WATERSPOUTS FROM UPPER AIR LAUNCH TOWER. WATERSPOUTS WERE NEARLY STATIONARY AND THE SECOND WATERSPOUT APPEARED TO BE FURTHER WEST THAN FIRST. SECOND WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED ABOUT 2 MINUTES. FIRST WATERSPOUT REMAINED VISIBLE FOR ANOTHER 4 MINUTES. TOTAL DURATION 10 MINUTES.

WATERSPOUT SPOTTED BY TRAINED SPOTTER JUST NORTH OF CUDJOE KEY. SPOTTER COULD NOT SEE THE HORIZION SO IT WAS UNKNOWN IF SPOUT HAD A SPRAY RING. SPOUT APPEARED AS A ROPE WITH A STRAIGHT UP-DOWN AXIS. RAIN SHAFT WAS CLOSE AND IT APPEARED TO BE GREY-BLUE COLOR. THERE WAS NO THUNDER/LIGHTNING NEAR THE SPOUT. THE SPOUT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY. SPOUT FORMED AT 1248 PM EST AND LASTED UNTIL 1256 PM EST, A TOTAL DURATION OF 8 MINUTES.

REPORT OF WATERSPOUT 10 MILES SE OF ISLAMORADA. SPOUT WAS JUST FORMING AT 0949 AM EDT. AT 1001 AM EDT EM CALLED BACK TO REPORT THE SPOUT HAD DEVELOPED A SPRAY RING WITH A STRAIGHT UP-DOWN TILT. NO RAIN SHAFTS OR THUNDER/LIGHTNING WERE NEARBY, THE SPOUT WAS CLEAR AND THEN BECAME MILKY WHITE IN COLOR AND APPEARED NEARLY STATIONARY. THE WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED AT 1008 AM EDT LASTING 19 MINUTES IN TOTAL.

THREE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED ALONG A CLOUD LINE FROM 540PM THROUGH 610PM ABOUT THREE MILES SOUTH OF KEY WEST.

MULTIPLE SPOTTERS CALLED IN TO REPORT A WATERSPOUT. SPOTTER REPORTED THAT THE FUNNEL WAS 1/2 WAY DOWN THE HORIZION, DID NOT HAVE A SPRAY RING, AND WAS BEGINNING TO ROPE OUT. THE SPOUT WAS TILTED GREATER THAN 30 DEGREES AND WAS CONSUMED BY A RAIN SHAFT NEARBY. THE SPOUT WAS GREY IN APPEARANCE AND THERE WAS NO LIGHTNING PRESENT. MOVEMENT OF THE SPOUT WAS TOWARD THE EAST AT ABOUT 3 MPH. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED AT 400 PM EDT AND DISSAPATED 6 MINUTES LATER.

TWO WATERSPOUTS REPORTED 4 MILES N OF MM 47. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE AT THE SURFACE. ONE SPOUT WAS RETREATING INTO THE CLOUD LINE WHILE THE OTHER SPOUT WAS A SKINNY ROPE ABOUT 1/3 OF THE WAY TO THE SURFACE AT 537PM EDT. THERE WAS A RAIN SHAFT TO THE EAST OF THE WATERSOUT. THERE WAS NO THUNDER OR LIGHTNING PRESENT. THE WATERSPOUTS WERE NEARLY STATIONARY DRIFTING EASTWARD. THE FIRST WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED AT 520PM EDT AND DECAYING WITH THE SECOND SPOUT STILL GOING AT 537 PM EDT.

A FUNNEL CLOUD WAS OBSERVED OVER FLORIDA BAY...ABOUT 4 MILES NORTH-NORTHWEST OF UPPER MATECUMBE KEY NEAR MILE MARKER 81. NO SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED. DURATION UNKNOWN. REPORT RELAYED BY MONROE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE.

PUBLIC PHONED IN A REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT AS THEY WERE DRIVING INTO WORK. PUBLIC REPORTED FUNNEL HALF WAY DOWN BUT COULD NOT SEE SPRAY RING DUE TO DISTANCE. LOCAL FORECASTERS IDENTIFIED CLOUD LINE AT A DISTANCE OF 5 MILES THAT COULD PRODUCE A WATERSPOUT...HOWEVER.
SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT WAS HALFWAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE, BUT QUICKLY RETREATED ALL IN THE SPAN OF A COUPLE OF MINUTES.

SPOTTER REPORTED WATERSPOUT 1 MILE OFFSHORE TO THE N OF MTH. SPRAY RING WAS ABOUT 60 FT HIGH. MEDIUM THICKNESS AND DARK GREY. THERE WAS NO RAIN SHAFT OR THUNDER/LIGHTNING. WATERSPOUT WAS DRIFITING EASTWARD SLOWLY. WATERSPOUT LIFE REPORTED FROM 335 TO 353 EDT.
AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ONE MILE SOUTH OF KEY WEST WITH A DURATION OF ONE MINUTE. WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE.
SPOTTER CALLED WITH REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT ON THE GULFSIDE WATERS NORTH OF MARATHON...VACA KEY.
SPOTTER REPORT...2ND REPORT...OF THE WATERSPOUT NORTH OF MARATHON...VACA KEY. WATERSPOUT TERMED SIGNIFICANT BY SPOTTER WITH A CLEARLY VISIBLE SPRAY RING. MOVEMENT TO THE SOUTH SOUTHWEST.

WATERSPOUT REPORTED 4 MILES OFFSHORE TO THE SOUTH OF DUCK KEY. SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. SPOUT WAS TILTED AND HAD A RAINSHAFT NEARBY. IT WAS MOVING E AT ABOUT 15 MPH. SPOUT LASTED 11 MINUTES.
